The main claim is: Ultimately, I write this post to express that Sam Altman’s phrase in “ Planning for AGI and beyond ”, “We believe that future of humanity should be determined by humanity”, is somehow deeply philosophically problematic and delusional.
Also, this would interfere with the eating habits of a lot of people and therefore such attempts would be very politically unpopular.

top_comment.author: avturchin
top_comment.text:
There are two important inputs in any mathematical AI alignment which need to be provided by humans or taken as un-said assumptions: who are "humans" and what is "human values" – or normatively important part of human values. AI can guess it, bit it needs AI Psychology and AI Sociology, which also could be biased.

Also see my short-form today about about reflexive stability of AI alignment.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: There are two important inputs in any mathematical AI alignment which need to be provided by humans or taken as un-said assumptions: who are "humans" and what is "human values" – or normatively important part of human values.

summary: The main claim is: Critics argue that the use of GPT-4 is invasive and puts everyone in a "perpetual police line-up." While there have been cases of mistaken identity using facial recognition, OpenAI claims a near 100% accuracy rate.

Critics argue that the police's use of the software is invasive and puts everyone in a "perpetual police line-up." Although the software is banned for most US companies, an exemption allows police to use it.

top_comment.author: Daniel Kokotajlo
top_comment.text:
Whoa, (a) you misspelled Anthropic, and also (b) you didn't change the name of the CEO. Everyone knows OpenAI's CEO is not Hoan Ton-That. I think GPT-4 could easily tell these articles are fake.

That said, cool cool. Maybe we should try to make this into a more rigorous study by getting lots of people (or LLMs?) to read the transcripts and judge bias / favorability / etc.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: I think GPT-4 could easily tell these articles are fake.

The main claim is: But my intuition tells me something different; I think of superintelligence in terms of complexity, eg, it has a greater ability to manage complexity than humans, and to us it is very complex.
I think complexity is a useful concept to think about, and it has an interesting characteristic, which is that it tends to escape your attempts to manage it, over time.

summary: The main claim is: Reinforcement agents do not, by default, "try" to maximize reward.
This claim is argued in the shard theory of human values.
The main claim is: People often talk about aligning language models, either to promote it, or to pooh-pooh it.
If you're brainstorming ideas for research aligning language models, I encourage you to think about connections between current language models and future AI that navigates the real world.

The main claim is: ELK's core idea is simple — get a model to truthfully report its own latent beliefs, as if it had 'drunk a truth serum' — and Hobbhahn considers the agenda very promising.
ELK differs from mainstream interpretability by targeting natural-language beliefs rather than low-level neurons/circuits, and by ARC's more theoretical style of attack.
